places.name: Agrasen ki Baoli
places.description: 14th-century historic stepwell known for its unique red-stone architecture and peaceful, mysterious vibe right in the middle of Delhi.

places.name: Janpath Market
places.description: Lively street market known for inexpensive clothing, Tibetan handicrafts, brass artifacts, and local souvenir shopping.

places.name: Central Park
places.description: Sprawling circular park at the center of Inner Circle, featuring a giant national flag, lush lawns, and walking paths.

places.name: Gurudwara Bangla Sahib
places.description: Prominent Sikh house of worship offering a tranquil pond (Sarovar), beautiful gold domes, and a community kitchen (Langar).

places.name: Jantar Mantar
places.description: Historic equinoctial sundial observatory built by Maharaja Jai Singh II of Jaipur in 1724.

places.name: Indian Coffee House
places.description: Classic, nostalgia-filled cafe in Mohan Singh Place serving filter coffee, masala dosas, and historical charm at low prices.

places.name: Cha Bar
places.description: Cozy cafe located inside Oxford Bookstore, offering hundreds of tea varieties, pastries, and a great reading environment.

places.name: Wenger's Deli
places.description: Historic bakery and deli famous for its mutton pastries, Swiss chocolates, and retro standing-only dining style.

places.name: Social Connaught Place
places.description: Energetic co-working space by day and high-energy pub by night, famous for innovative cocktails and quirky food presentation.

places.name: Ministry of Beer
places.description: Delhi's first steam-punk themed craft brewery in CP, serving delicious microbrewed beers and multi-cuisine pub bites.

places.name: Lord of the Drinks
places.description: Spacious lounge bar with a grand medieval theme and an outdoor terrace overlooking the circular streets of CP.

places.name: Saravana Bhavan
places.description: Vegetarian restaurant serving authentic South Indian filter coffee, giant ghee rava dosas, and standard thalis.

places.name: United Coffee House
places.description: Grand, high-ceilinged colonial era dining lounge serving signature blends, Indian classics, and continental favorites since 1942.

places.name: Palika Bazaar
places.description: Underground air-conditioned market known for clothes, electronics, and local bargaining experiences.

places.name: Hauz Khas Fort
places.description: 13th-century fort ruins with a beautiful reservoir, ancient mosque, tomb, and madrasa, offering sunset views.

places.name: Deer Park
places.description: Lush green oasis adjoining the fort, featuring spotted deer, peacocks, walking trails, and dense foliage.

places.name: Hauz Khas Lake
places.description: Historic water reservoir built by Alauddin Khalji, surrounded by paths and popular for bird watching and walking.

places.name: Social Hauz Khas
places.description: Lakeside outpost of the famous chain with retro-industrial decor, serving craft beer and creative fusion snacks.

places.name: Kunzum Travel Cafe
places.description: Quirky travel-themed cafe where travelers share stories, browse travel photography, and pay whatever they want for coffee.

places.name: The Tea Room by Blossom Kochhar
places.description: Charming, elegant cafe in Hauz Khas Village serving premium organic teas, coffees, and delicious pastries.

places.name: Coast Cafe
places.description: Light-filled coastal restaurant specializing in Kerala-style curry, appams, fish tacos, and great cocktails.

places.name: Yeti - The Himalayan Kitchen
places.description: Acclaimed restaurant serving authentic Tibetan, Nepalese, and Bhutanese dishes like momos, thukpa, and shapta.

places.name: Mia Bella
places.description: Rooftop dining restaurant overlooking the lake, famous for Italian fare, cocktails, and sunset views.

places.name: Naivedyam
places.description: Beautiful traditional-style restaurant serving high-quality South Indian meals with warm service.

places.name: Summer House Cafe
places.description: Vibrant bar and nightclub nearby offering live music gigs, rooftop seating, and a bustling weekend nightlife scene.

places.name: Hauz Khas Village Boutiques
places.description: Labyrinth of narrow lanes filled with independent fashion designer shops, antique stores, and quirky art galleries.

places.name: Safdarjung Tomb
places.description: 18th-century sandstone and marble mausoleum showcasing the grandeur of Late Mughal architecture.

places.name: Safdarjung Development Area (SDA) Market
places.description: Vibrant market hub popular with university students, lined with hip cafes, dessert shops, and street vendors.

places.name: The Piano Man Jazz Club
places.description: Renowned jazz club offering nightly live performances, cozy seating, craft cocktails, and a distinct speakeasy vibe.

places.name: Rajinder Da Dhaba
places.description: Iconic local eatery famous for its delicious and spicy North Indian curries, kebabs, and Galouti rolls.

places.name: What A Comic Show
places.description: Charming comic-book themed cafe serving great coffee, burgers, and Italian dishes in a relaxed environment.

places.name: Humayunpur Market
places.description: Narrow, bustling lanes famous for Northeast Indian food joints, boutique shopping, and trendy fashion outlets.

places.name: Cultured Cafe
places.description: Hip coffee shop offering a serene atmosphere, delicious sourdough toasts, and pour-over coffee specialties.

places.name: Lodi Gardens
places.description: Lush historic park located nearby, dotted with Sayyid and Lodi dynasty tombs, popular for morning jogs.

places.name: Mizo Diner
places.description: Cozy restaurant in Humayunpur specializing in authentic Mizo cuisine, smoked pork dishes, and bamboo shoot curries.

places.name: Hawa Mahal
places.description: The iconic 'Palace of Winds', a five-story pink sandstone pyramid-like facade built in 1799.

places.name: City Palace Jaipur
places.description: Magnificent royal residence showcasing a blend of Rajput and Mughal architecture, with museums and courtyards.

places.name: Jantar Mantar Jaipur
places.description: UNESCO World Heritage site featuring nineteen architectural astronomical instruments built by Sawai Jai Singh II.

places.name: Johri Bazar
places.description: Traditional jewelry market famous for gold, silver, emeralds, and handmade Bandhej block-print textiles.

places.name: Bapu Bazar
places.description: Vibrant market lane known for Rajasthani leather shoes (mojris), handicraft home decor, and traditional block prints.

places.name: Laxmi Mishthan Bhandar (LMB)
places.description: Legendary sweet shop and dining room famous for its Rajasthani thali, Pyaaz Kachori, and Ghewar sweet.

places.name: Wind View Cafe
places.description: Rooftop cafe located directly opposite Hawa Mahal, offering great views of the palace facade and classic masala chai.

places.name: Lassiwala
places.description: Famous shop on MI Road serving thick, sweet lassi in traditional clay cups (kulhads) since 1944.

places.name: Bar Palladio
places.description: Stunning heritage-style restaurant and lounge featuring royal blue interiors and classical Italian food.

places.name: Bandra-Worli Sea Link
places.description: Stunning cable-stayed bridge spanning the Arabian Sea, best viewed from the Bandra coastline.

places.name: Bandstand Promenade
places.description: Scenic seaside walking pathway popular for sea views, matching breeze, and spotting Bollywood stars' homes.

places.name: Mount Mary Church
places.description: Historic Roman Catholic Basilica of Our Lady of the Mount, situated on a hill overlooking the ocean.

places.name: Bandra Fort
places.description: Castella de Aguada, a historic 17th-century Portuguese watchtower offering panoramic sea views at sunset.

places.name: Carter Road Promenade
places.description: Lively coastal boulevard featuring a culinary strip, walking tracks, and musical performances.

places.name: Linking Road
places.description: Famous shopping street offering a mix of brand outlets, affordable footwear stalls, and local street wear.

places.name: Hill Road
places.description: Bustling retail street lined with thrift shops, export-surplus clothing outlets, and local accessories.

places.name: Candies
places.description: Iconic Portuguese-villa style cafe with multi-level terrace seating, known for chicken rolls, lasagna, and wild berry iced tea.

places.name: Salt Water Cafe
places.description: Chic, relaxed restaurant serving European bistro fare, fresh seafood, and outstanding sangrias.

places.name: Toto's Garage
places.description: Famous VW Beetle-themed pub with auto body parts hanging from the ceiling, playing classic rock and serving chilled beer.

places.name: Taj Mahal Tea House
places.description: Elegant tea lounge set in an old Bandra bungalow, offering high-end artisanal teas, classical music, and local snacks.

places.name: Shibuya Crossing
places.description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing, an iconic symbol of Tokyo's neon-lit urban energy.

places.name: Meiji Jingu Shrine
places.description: A tranquil Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji, located inside a sprawling, dense forest in Shibuya.

places.name: Shibuya Sky
places.description: A breathtaking open-air observation deck on top of Shibuya Scramble Square, offering panoramic views of Tokyo and Mount Fuji.

places.name: Ichiran Shibuya
places.description: Famous tonkotsu ramen chain where diners eat in individual semi-private booths, customizing their broth and noodles.

places.name: Hachiko Statue
places.description: The bronze statue commemorating the legendary loyal dog Hachiko, located right outside Shibuya Station.

places.name: Shibuya Nonbei Yokocho
places.description: 'Drunkards Alley', a narrow pathway lined with tiny, rustic yakitori bars preserving post-war Tokyo atmosphere.

places.name: Chatei Hatou
places.description: An exquisite, old-school kissaten coffee shop renowned for artisanal hand-drip coffee and beautiful hand-chosen ceramic cups.

places.name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
places.description: One of Tokyo's largest parks, blending traditional Japanese, English landscape, and French formal garden styles.

places.name: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building
places.description: Dual-tower government building offering free panoramic observatories on the 45th floor, overlooking the Tokyo skyline.

places.name: Omoide Yokocho
places.description: 'Memory Lane', a tiny network of alleys lined with open-grill yakitori stands and rustic noodle shops.

places.name: Golden Gai
places.description: A collection of six narrow alleys filled with over 200 tiny bars, each seating only a few people, known for their unique themes.

places.name: Blue Bottle Coffee Shinjuku
places.description: Sleek, minimalist coffee shop serving fresh, custom pour-overs inside the bustling NewoMan shopping complex.

places.name: Senso-ji Temple
places.description: Tokyo's oldest and most famous Buddhist temple, dedicated to the Bodhisattva Kannon, featuring a grand red lantern gate.

places.name: Nakamise-dori Shopping Street
places.description: Bustling, centuries-old market approach to Senso-ji temple, selling traditional snacks (senbei, ningyo-yaki) and souvenirs.

places.name: Daikokuya Tempura
places.description: Historic restaurant operating since 1887, famous for its dark-sauce tempura bowls served on traditional tatami floors.

places.name: Kamiya Bar
places.description: Tokyo's first Western-style bar, open since 1880, famous for its signature potent herbal cocktail 'Denki Bran'.

places.name: Suzukien Asakusa
places.description: Matcha specialty shop famous for serving the world's most intense matcha gelato, graded from level 1 to level 7.

places.name: Eiffel Tower
places.description: The world-famous iron lattice monument, offering panoramic vistas of Paris from its three public viewing platforms.

places.name: Champ de Mars
places.description: Large public greenspace directly underneath the Eiffel Tower, perfect for picnics, casual walks, and viewing the evening light show.

places.name: Rue Cler
places.description: One of Paris's most famous market streets, lined with cheese shops, bakeries, cafes, and gourmet specialty boutiques.

places.name: Café de l'Homme
places.description: Premium terrace cafe at Trocadero, offering elegant bistro snacks and a direct, head-on view of the Eiffel Tower.

places.name: Les Ombres
places.description: Rooftop restaurant situated on top of the Musée du Quai Branly, serving refined French cuisine under a glass roof.

places.name: Louvre Museum
places.description: The world's largest art museum, housing iconic masterpieces like the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo within a former royal palace.

places.name: Tuileries Garden
places.description: Historic formal French garden between the Louvre and Place de la Concorde, featuring fountains, sculpture, and walkways.

places.name: Café Marly
places.description: Elegant arcade cafe looking directly onto the Louvre's iconic glass pyramid, perfect for espresso or cocktails.

places.name: Angelina Paris
places.description: Famous belle-epoque tea room on Rue de Rivoli, renowned for its decadent, thick hot chocolate and Mont-Blanc pastry.

places.name: Le Meurice Alain Ducasse
places.description: Two-Michelin-starred restaurant offering exceptional, high-end French gastronomy inside an ornate, palace-style dining room.

places.name: Sacré-Cœur Basilica
places.description: The Roman Catholic basilica crowning the hill of Montmartre, offering outstanding views of the entire Paris skyline.

places.name: Place du Tertre
places.description: The central artistic square where painters, portraitists, and caricaturists set up their easels daily for tourists.

places.name: Café des Deux Moulins
places.description: The classic Parisian corner bistro made world-famous as the workplace of Amélie Poulain in the movie 'Amélie'.

places.name: Le Consulat
places.description: Iconic, highly photographed historic restaurant on the hill, serving traditional French dishes (escargots, steak frites).

places.name: La Cigale
places.description: Legendary cabaret-theater and concert hall at the base of the hill, hosting live indie bands and comedy performances.

places.name: Empire State Building
places.description: The iconic 102-story Art Deco skyscraper in Midtown, offering 360-degree views of New York City and beyond.

places.name: The High Line
places.description: A 1.45-mile elevated public park and boardwalk built on a historic, abandoned freight rail line on the West Side.

places.name: Chelsea Market
places.description: A bustling indoor food hall and retail market in the Meatpacking District, housed inside a former Nabisco factory.

places.name: Katz's Delicatessen
places.description: Legendary kosher-style deli on the Lower East Side, famous for its hand-carved, piled-high pastrami sandwiches since 1888.

places.name: Stumptown Coffee Roasters
places.description: Hip, specialty coffee shop located in the lobby of the Ace Hotel, serving rich pour-overs and cold brews.

places.name: Brooklyn Bridge
places.description: The historic neo-Gothic suspension bridge, offering a scenic walk over the East River with views of the skyline.

places.name: DUMBO Waterfront
places.description: Waterfront park and streets offering the classic framed view of the Manhattan Bridge between red-brick warehouses.

places.name: Brooklyn Roasting Company
places.description: Spacious, industrial-style coffee roaster in DUMBO serving excellent espresso blends and local pastries.

places.name: Grimaldi's Pizzeria
places.description: Famous coal-fired brick-oven pizzeria underneath the Brooklyn Bridge, known for its thin-crust Neapolitan pies.

places.name: Brooklyn Flea
places.description: A massive outdoor flea market featuring hundreds of vendors selling vintage clothing, furniture, and local crafts.

places.name: Times Square Red Steps
places.description: The iconic red stairs on top of the TKTS booth, offering a prime vantage point of the flashing billboard canyon.

places.name: Broadway Theater District
places.description: The world-famous cluster of professional theaters hosting award-winning musicals and dramatic plays.

places.name: Junior's Restaurant
places.description: Classic diner famous for its legendary, dense New York cheesecake, Reuben sandwiches, and extensive diner menu.

places.name: Ellen's Stardust Diner
places.description: Retro 1950s-themed diner featuring a world-famous staff of singing waiters who perform Broadway hits during service.

places.name: Bethesda Fountain & Terrace
places.description: The heart of Central Park, featuring a grand neoclassical terrace, lakeside underpass acoustic vault, and fountain.

places.name: Central Park Zoo
places.description: Charming, historic zoological park located near the southeast corner of the park, housing penguins, seals, and snow leopards.

places.name: The Loeb Boathouse Cafe
places.description: Lakeside cafe and deck where you can watch rowboats glide by while enjoying coffee and pastries.

places.name: Tavern on the Green
places.description: A historic, upscale restaurant set inside a former sheep fold, offering elegant American fare and garden seating.
